column11: 1200: Barometer 1440 made by Geo. Green was fitted. Noon: Portsmouth NH
2000: Aneroid and Mercurial barometers not ready for use.
1230: Got Underway. Moved anchorage to off Fort Constitution
0845: Got Underway. Out of Lower Hbr Portsmouth NH.
0915: Whale back LH brg East, Shaped course S1/2E, 0930 White Id Lh bore East 2mi cc SxE1/2E. This time is wrong.

column29: 2000: Watch ended with most beutiful Aurora rising in the NW and extending to the height of about 75 deg forming an arc ending in the ExN pt of the horizon. Cape Cod Lt brg a 1/2 Pt forward of bow 8mi 2000: Brilliant Aurora Borealis in the NW

column11: 1030: Experimented in turning ship in a circle with stbd helm.
1330: After manouvering to avoid a vessel regained course WxN
0735: Sandy Hook LS W1/2S dist 5 mi.
0800: Helm hard to stbd to turn ship.
1200: Standing into Narrows opposite Yellow Fever quarantine stn. 1245 came to. Brgs E end of Ft Wadsworth SxE1/2E, E end of Ft Lafayette SE, Robbins reef Lt N1/2E Noon: New York
1030: HalF masted ensign and jack on account of the death of the President of the US.
1720: At 1720 Remarkable rise in Barometer noted on page

column28: 1000: The vessels of the North Atlantic got underway.
Top of Page: USS Vaudalia, USS saratoga
Top of Page: USS Kearsarge anchored

column11: 1205: Sandy hook Ls close aboard, shaped course SE1/4S

column29: 2230: Noticed a very bright meteor, brg SSE

0230: Saw a beautiful meteor in the weston horizon, when first seen it was about 25 deg height going to the eastward, when nearing the horizon it exploded, breaking into three pieces and showing blue & red.

2330: Observed a brilliant meteor, having a greenish apperance, it appeared on Northwestern sky about 40 deg above horizon, passing in an Easterly direction, it disappeared in North Eastern sky at an elevation of about 15 deg

column11: 1558: Anchored. Greta Point SWxS, Magdalcua Rk ExS, ????, SW point of Pico SSW
1215: Sighted E end of St Michael Id brg SxE1/2E
0230: se1/2e, 0250 Cape St Vincent Lt bore N
1700: SExE, 1740 Trafalgar Lt brg NE1/2N Cape Spartel brg SSE,1750 cc SSE1/2E
Noon: Gibraltar. I changed baro 30.22 to 30.32.

column29: 2000: (2000-2400 watch no specific time given) Saw a bright meteor going to the SW

column11: 1200: Point De Gat Lt bore N35E
Noon: Carthageua Hbr Spain  (I assume that is Cartagena)
2250: Sighted Coneira Lt brg E1/2S  (Conillera Lighthouse ?)
1600: Id of Majorca bore off Stbd qtr.
2335: Cape San Sabastian Lt bore abm (Cape de Sant Sebastia ??)

column29: 1700: Near total eclipse of the moon at 1700, ended about 1930.

column11: 0820: Got Underway. Passed out of Hbr for target practice
